entry.title: Re: PPDS on SAP GUI for Java known issues
entry.content: This is the SAP Note regarding that issue https://me.sap.com/notes/0002616384

entry.title: My intro
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,
My name is Faith and I’m excited to be a part of this SAP Learning Community! A little about myself: I’m originally from NJ but I am based out in TX. I graduated from Rutgers University with a bachelor’s degree in information systems, in 2020. I’m currently working towards my SAP Certified Associate - Implementation Consultant - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with the Vower learning group, and I’m really looking forward to expanding my knowledge in business processes and implementation and regulations.
I’m particularly excited about obtaining this certification because it will help my career path in becoming a GRC analyst & expand my knowledge in other areas of IT. I’m also open to connecting with others who are interested in cybersecurity or the GRC side of cybersecurity. Looking forward to learning and growing with all of you!
Best regards,

entry.title: Re: Langchain Summarization Techniques: Leveraging Generative AI Hub via Custom Model
entry.content: Insightful

entry.title: Re: LMS User connector SF getting failed
entry.content: @BS181, it's possible to review if an FTP/SFTP is being used to place the file and if the file is fully available in the FTP/SFTP when Learning is scheduled to run (Export Cautions for User Connector - SF).

entry.title: Re: Group Reporting Rule Based COI - TP calculation
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I have the same issue, when I run the task 2100 the system (S4 HANA 1909) doesn't post non controlling interest, however there are companies with a lower percentage than 100%  and with purchase method as consolidation method

These are the percentage selection, the upload percentages and the defined task,

 

Please, could you help me?

Thanks,

Best regards

entry.title: Re: Quering an OData Endpoint with a timestamp does not w...
entry.content:
Hi @jensniemeyer ,

Have you tried the SQL-like fluent API mentioned in the documentation?

I'm thinking something like this:

 

SELECT(["AccountID", "ContactID"]).from("ContactCollection").where({ ChangedOn: { '>': myDate } })

 

Could you check if this could work?

Rgards,

Peter

entry.title: Re: The value 'null' cannot be set as 'From' address in t...
entry.content:
Hi @rautrkai ,

It's not entirely clear to me, but from the error message and your description, I think the solution might be to make sure that the value in From is a valid email, but at least not empty or null, before calling the send email routine.

I hope this helps.

Regards,

Peter

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Dear David,

Thank you for the opportunity that SAP is providing to participate in SAP Learning’s Digital Skills Initiative!

I am having more than 10+ years of professional experience in SAP ERP and now I would like to enhance my career opportunities becoming a Certified SAP Consultant.

My learning Journey starts from the course SAP Data Analyst – SAP Analytics Cloud and to complete Certification I have to pass next exams:

SAP Certified Associate - Data Analyst - SAP Analytics Cloud

SAP Certified Application Associate - SAP Business One Release 10.0

SAP Certified Specialist - SAP Activate for Cloud Solutions Project Manager.

I would be very thankful for you to attend Certification Exams using the possibility provided by Digital Skills Initiative.

Thank you!

Best Regards,

Lidiia Koltsova

entry.title: Re: Certify Your Skills as SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Implementation Consultant
entry.content:
Hi Dieter,

It's absolutely clear for me. If SAP as ERP solution provider or the other companies that need to be implemented SAP Public Cloud requiring SAP Public Cloud certification as tender material consider that the acknowledgment of Public Cloud certification for other LoBs only uses certification of C_S4CPB plus RoA as your explanation, then we will follow the update information. However I think the best way for SAP Public Cloud certification that SAP should provide for each LoB as available solution currently, such as Asset Management, Manufacturing, Professional Services, etc. Because C_S4CPB may be too general for the other LoBs.

Thanks for your response beforehand.

entry.title: To create a new wage for deduction of HRR which is not considered for housing perk calculation.
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I am looking your help regarding a query. please go through the following issue received from client and let me know how to do. if possible please guide me with screen shots, it will be helpful to me as future reference.

QUERY:  

In respect of employee no. xxxxx and by maintaining wage type 2054 for deduction of HRR of Rs. 3040 p.m.., Housing Perks is not required .
However, by maintaining wage type 2054 in other case, Housing Perks is ok even system is also fine.
In view of above, it is requested to kindly create new sub wage type for deduction of HRR in case of CVO only so that the correct pay slip ( Gross - Deductions ) may be issued.
Now the client requesting to create a new wage for deduction of HRR which is not considered for housing perk calculation.
 
Regards...
Ananthj

samer.alhato@tronox.com

entry.title: Re: Extensibility for Manage Prices - Sales
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

 

We have a requirement to trigger the workflow based on pricing communication data. We created the workflow and implemented Badi SD_APM_SET_APPROVAL_REASON but it doesn't have pricing communication as importing and even standard CDS available for pricing will not return the pricing data during first time create of sales order as it is not created yet. This blog says about the data to be transferred from Order Item to Pricing, however is there any business process that would pass on the data from pricing communication to item of the order. Just FYI, order item has subtotal fields available to aggregate the values from the pricing procedure but all of them are occupied. Unfortunately, I couldn't find any solution for this yet. Have anyone came across such requirement and able to crack, request your inputs on it.

Best,

HP

entry.title: Release strategy for Purchase Order not working
entry.content:
Hello expert,
I've been trying to implement a Purchase Order Release Strategy to see if it works. It should trigger when INCO1 = CFR and PO Type = It is not working.

Can you please help me out? I'll attach my screens.

 

Thanks so much in advance!

entry.title: Requirement to trigger the workflow based on pricing communication data during Sales Order Create
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

 

We have a requirement to trigger the workflow based on pricing communication data. We created the workflow and implemented Badi SD_APM_SET_APPROVAL_REASON but it doesn't have pricing communication as importing and even standard CDS available for pricing will not return the pricing data during first time create of sales order as it is not created yet. There are blog that says about the data to be transferred from Order Item to Pricing, however is there any business process that would pass on the data from pricing communication to item of the order. Just FYI, order item has subtotal fields available to aggregate the values from the pricing procedure but all of them are occupied. Unfortunately, I couldn't find any solution for this yet. Have anyone came across such requirement and able to crack, request your inputs on it.

Best,

HP

entry.title: Guidance on Transitioning from SAP Business One Consultant to SAP S/4HANA FICO consultant
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I have 10 years of experience working with SAP Business One and am now transitioning to become an SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud FICO consultant. Could you kindly guide me on how to correlate the key differences between SAP Business One and S/4HANA, particularly in terms of functionality, additional features, and configuration tasks in FICO?

Furthermore, if there are any relevant documents or study materials available, I would greatly appreciate it if you could share them.

Thank you for your support.

Best regards,
Silambu

entry.title: Connecting to a RAC Database in SAP Integration Suite: A Step-by-Step Guide Using JDBC Adapter
entry.content:
Connecting to a Cluster Database (RAC) in Suite: A Challenge and Solution

The limitations of direct connections to RAC databases in Suite by leveraging HTTP calls through PI to CPI. This innovative approach provides a flexible and reliable solution for integrating with clustered databases.

Prerequisites:

  • Cluster Database (RAC): Ensure you have a properly configured RAC database instance running.
  • SAP Integration Suite: Have SAP Integration Suite installed and configured.
  • PI (Process Integration): Have PI installed and configured to communicate with your RAC database.
  • CPI (Cloud Platform Integration): Have CPI set up and configured to interact with PI and the RAC database.
  • HTTP Client: You'll need an HTTP client tool (e.g., Postman, curl) to make the necessary HTTP calls.

Process:

IFlow:

Fetch the table data: Content modifier property details

Content Modifier Message Body

 ${property.Http_Select}

 

Http_Select: REST calls support both JSON and XML, so the HTTP input format for this integration should be XML. The table and query information should be included in the REST input as XML elements.

The SELECT query should be defined in the access element, while the table name should be specified in the action element.

<root>
<stmt>
<TableName action="SQL_QUERY">
<access>Select fieldnames from tablename and rownum = 1</access>
</TableName>
</stmt>
</root>

Http Adapter:

HttpAddress: http://<PIHost>:<PIPort>/RESTAdapter/Call_PI/Send_JDBC

To establish a connection between the Cloud Connector and the on-premises PI system, the following information must be configured:

  • PI Host and Port: These details are essential for the Cloud Connector to locate and communicate with the on-premises PI system.
  • Location ID and PI Credentials: These credentials are required to authenticate and authorize the Cloud Connector's access to the PI system.
  • REST Call Address: The same HTTP address should be used in the REST call within PI to ensure proper communication between the two systems.

Pass through ICO in PI:

REST Call:

JDBC Call: A service-based connection to an Oracle DB running on Oracle RAC (cluster).

Groovy Script: replace root node

To align the structure of the CPI response with the JDBC response.

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message;
import java.util.HashMap;
def Message processData(Message message) {
//Read Input as String
def body = message.getBody(String);

//Replace XML Root and Sub Node
String output = body.replace("root_response", "ROOT").replace("stmt_response", "select_response");

//Set Output
message.setBody(output);
return message;
}

Payload difference before and after groovy

Before groovy:

After groovy:

Following the Groovy script, the integration flow can proceed to the next step based on the script's output and the defined requirements.

NOTE:SAP has recommended the Edge Integration Cell as the ideal solution for this requirement within SAP Integration Suite. However, due to our tight timeline, we're exploring a more immediate option using SAP PO, which already has established connections.

Bhavani.

entry.title: Re: Decimals Issue in FIORI List Report

entry.title: Re: LSMW for Functional Consultants in simple step-by-step way
entry.content:
Very good Guide

So helpfully

Tank you

entry.title: Re: What are OfflineOdata Headers and its different types...
entry.content:
Hi @daniyal_dolare,

Transaction.Ignore is SSAM specific flag used in "Headers".

SSAM backend will ignore the transactions if the flag is set.

"Transaction.Ignore": "true" will ignore the transaction &
"Transaction.Ignore": "false" will process the transaction normally.

If the header is not present, the assumed value of "Transaction.Ignore" is "false" for regular processing.

E.g. The confirmation overview rows are only for the frontend to show the 14 days entries. Backend doesn't need to process them so they will be ignored.

SSAM frontend metadata will be creating a transaction with "Transaction.Ignore": "true" in "Headers" just to satisfy any OData Constraints or MDK limitation or client only purposes.

~ Sincerely Arun

entry.title: Exporting contents creates an Hash
entry.content:
Hi 
When exporting a Package from SAP Integration Suite. It creates an Hash file containing to Hash's

How are these Hashes computed - from what content ?

An example is attached.

 The hash looks like a SHA-256 but from what is it created ?

entry.title: Re: What are OfflineOdata Headers and its different types...
entry.content:
Hi @daniyal_dolare,

Regarding "OfflineOData.TransactionID", it is used when you need to send multiple transactions in same batch. Refer help documentation here. Learn more about OData v2 batch processing.

Regarding "OfflineOData.RemoveAfterUpload": "true", will delete the entry from offline store(udb) after successfully uploaded in the back end. New parameter is removeCreatedEntityAfterUpload.

Regarding "OfflineOData.NonMergeable": true, will prevent transaction merging. For eg. If you save a form, its state may change from 'New' to 'In Process' to 'Completed' and this way those state transitions are preserved in multiple transactions rather than being merged into a single transaction.

Regarding "Transaction.Ignore", It is SSAM specific flag used in offline odata "Headers".

SSAM backend will ignore the transactions if the flag is set.

"Transaction.Ignore": "true" will ignore the transaction &
"Transaction.Ignore": "false" will process the transaction normally.

If the header is not present, the assumed value of "Transaction.Ignore" is "false" for regular processing.

E.g. The confirmation overview rows are only for the frontend to show the 14 days entries. Backend doesn't need to process them so they will be ignored.

SSAM frontend metadata will be creating a transaction with "Transaction.Ignore": "true" in "Headers" to satisfy any OData Constraints or MDK limitation or client only purposes.

Note: "Headers" section is for developer to specify their own custom HTTP headers that their backend can recognise. By default "Headers" are empty.

~ Sincerely Arun

entry.title: How to call a CPI Artifact (REST / SOAP API) from SAP API Management
entry.content:
Pre requisites for this blog

  • Activate - Manage API capability (if not activated yet)
  • Setup API Business Hub Enterprise and add your user as Developer

Introduction

In this blog, I am going to use a public calculator SOAP service as an example and create a SOAP API as CPI artifact then call the same SOAP API from SAP API Management. So, let's begin.  

Step # 1: Create a SOAP API

  • Create a new package or edit an existing one (I am creating a new package with name "Calculator")
  • Add a new artifact, choose API as artifact type.

  • A popup window will appear, asking for the runtime profile information choose Cloud Integration and click Next
  • On the next screen, select "SOAP API" as API type and click Next
  • Choose New and click Next
  • Give API an name (in my case, i name it as "Calculator API" ) and click Create

A new SOAP API will be created inside the package, open the API in edit mode and delete Content Modifier from Handle Request - Local Integration Process. 

 

Step # 2: Edit the Calculator API 

Note: I am going to use dneonline (open the link and save the WSDL in your local drive for later use)web service in this example.

  • edit the "SOAP" as sender adapter setting
    • give an address to the SOAP API 
    • select WSDL in "Service Definition" and browse the WSDL you have save earlier. Once you select the WSDL choose operation you want to perform.

  • Add "Request-Reply" step in Internal integration process and connect it with receiver and choose "SOAP" as receiver adapter type.

  • Open SOAP setting (as receiver adapter) and edit as below

  • Save and Deploy the SOAP API

 Step # 3: Test SOAP API

Once API is deployed, download the WSDL definition from Monitor > Integrations & APIs. Go to SoapUI and browse the newly saved WSDL definition. Choose Add operation and test the service. (Note: for authentication use integration-flow type service key credentials) 

Step # 4: Create a Provider as Type "Cloud Integrations"

Note: Copy the "API" type service key information (open BTP Cockpit and go to subscription page there locate "Instances" table, download/copy the key information.

First, we need to create an API Provider for that: 

  • go-to "Configure >> APIs" 
  • then go-to "API Providers" tab
  • and click "Create" 
  • Type name as "CPI Provider" and move to next tab "Connection"
  • Select "Cloud Integrations"
  • Open the downloaded key file and copy the URL 
  • Paste it in "Cloud Integration Management Host" field
  • select Authentication type as "OAuth2ClientCredentials"
  • Copy ClientID and Client-Secret and paste respectively.
  • Save and Test the connection

Step # 5: Create a API Proxy using CPI Provider

  • Go to Configure > APIs, this time select "API Proxies" tab
  • Click "Create"
  • A new popup window will appears, select API Provider in radio list
  • and choose the newly created CPI Provider from the drop-down list and click "Discover"
  • A new dialog will open, where you will find the Calculator API we created in Step 1, choose it and click "Next"
  • For authentication choose "Basic" and input the credentials which you have use to test the SOAP API in step 3 and click "Done"
  • Once you back to the API Create window you will see few fields are already filled in, you can change the text if wish to but keep the URL value as it is, i am keeping them as it is. 

  • Click "Create" to create the Proxy
  • On the next screen, click "Save" and then "Deploy".

  • Copy the API Proxy URL 

Step # 6: Test the API Proxy

  • Open SoapUI and edit the endpoint on the request we testing in Step 3
  • set the authentication to "No Authentication" and click "Submit" 

Step # 7: Apply Policy to Secure the API

  • Edit the API Proxy created step 5
  • and go to Policies 
  • On the next screen select "Edit"
  • On the left hand side Policy Edit expand the "Proxy Endpoint" and select "PreFlow"
  • Then choose Verify API Key policy (click plus sign) from right hand side of the side menu 

  • Give a name to the policy and click "Add"

  • update the variable reference name to  "request.header.apikey"

  • click "Update" button 
  • and then Save and Deploy again

Step # 8: Create a Product and Subscribe it

  • To create a product go to Engage 

  • Keep on Products Tab and click "Create"
  • Type name and title and move to "APIs" tab
  • Click "Add" and select the Calculator API Proxy from the list and Click OK
  • Publish the Product
  • Now go to API Business Hub Enterprise 

  • You will see the product you created earlier there
  • select the product and go to next page
  • To subscribe choose New application from the drop-down list
  • Give a name and click Create 

  • Now, copy the key for testing 

Step #9: Test API with API Key

  • Go to SoapUI again
  • add a header as "apikey" and paste the copied key in value field 
  • now submit the request you will receive the response 

Conclusion

In this way, you can secure your CPI artifacts from direct access. SAP API Management offers lot of different policies which help developer built a security layer around the backend services. I just added the authentication mechanism, but it offers much more such as Traffic Management Policies, Security Management Policies, Mediation Policies.

entry.title: Feedback about SAP TechEd 24 "portal" - Virtual registration
entry.content:
Hi mates,

I would like to thanks SAP, sponsors and partner for achieving the release of the SAP TechEd 24 virtual edition.
The portal with dashboard is really great, especially the search engine and the "tag" feature to browse through contents with a "product" oriented view and track type !

Thanks

Amaury

entry.title: Re: trace all queries coming for specific database
entry.content:
Hi,

Please check the below and see which one is feasible to you in your case.

1.Using MDA tables 
   select * from monProcessSQLText
   select * from monSysSQLText

2. You can enable auditing to capture SQL text by turning it on to for each login/object you want to monitor. Please see the documentation under www.help.sap.com for auditing.

3. Using dbcc commands for SQL text and from sp_w/sp_who/master..sysprocesses for the info.
dbcc traceon (3604)
dbcc sqltext(<spid>)

Regards,
Dilip Voora

entry.title: Re: Fiori attachments storage into OpenText archive
entry.content:
Hello,

there are indeed more and more Fiori Apps that are storing attachments as DMS objects.

This means that DIRs (Document Info Record) are automatically created by the system and are:

- On one hand connected to the object on which you are adding attachments

- On the other hand storing the attachment

You can use CV04N to retrieve the list of created DIRs. There if you open one at the bottom of the first screen you'll see the attachments. The important element there, to find out where they are physically stored, is the Storage Cat. attribute = Storage Category.

Once you have identified the Storage Category, just go in transaction OACT to retrieve it; there you can also see the corresponding content repository, define in OAC0 and that can be database storage, OpenText content server etc. 

In OACT you can change the content repository so that the Storage Category used in the DIRs is well going to your desired content server.

In DMS configuration you can also do the changes to use another storage category...

Rgds

entry.title: Re: Store attachments from Standard Fiori apps in Archive...
entry.content:
Hi,

Indeed HDM is restricted to few Standard Fiori apps only. SAP shall very probably add more and more Apps over the next releases but of course we don't know how many releases it will take before most of it is covered.

Also note that some Apps, and Dispute Management is one of them, are storing attachments yet in a slighlty different way technically.

entry.title: Re: Vendor notes and Attachments data migration from ECC...
entry.content:
Simplest is to use some Z programs.

One on the ERP will extract all attachments and store them on a file system keeping the file name and the object to which they are connected in the ERP.

The other imports all files in S/4 adding attachments directly to the corresponding objects. If the object keys are different you should use a mapping table.

This is something I have often done in various environments.

Other possibility is to run the Z program on S/4 only and allow RFC connection to the ERP in order to extract and transfer attachments without going through a temporary file system. This requires however that RFC connection from S/4 to ERP is possible and allowed. Can be a bit more challenging if S/4 is in the cloud...

entry.title: Re: How to map the Archive server to Kpro in SAP S4 DMS
entry.content:
In DMS configuration, check the Kpro 'on'

In OACT change the storage category to make it point to the content repository you want to use. The default storage category is also set in the DMS configuration.

entry.title: Re: About the default condition types of item in Sales order
entry.content: Hi Jason, Please check the pricing procedure assigned to Sales area/ customer pricing procedure/ Document pricing procedure ... all the seen condition types will be assigned in that .. for corresponding values, either check material master or pricing condition records maintained. .. thank you

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:

I hope you're doing well. My name is Nauman, and I am a Chartered Accountancy (CA) student from Pakistan. I am currently pursuing a career shift towards SAP and have been actively engaged in learning about SAP systems and implementations. I believe the Digital Skills Initiative would be a great opportunity for me, especially since I come from a region where access to certain technology career resources is limited.

I am eager to participate in this initiative, complete a relevant learning journey, and work towards SAP certification. 

Thank you so much for this initiative and for supporting underrepresented individuals.

entry.title: Re: Why Failure-Mode Focus Matters in Intelligent Asset Management
entry.content:
Hi,

Thanks for for your feedback and thoughts.

Manage Strategy Assessments for Classes is following the intent to streamline assessments and address multiple assets - instead doing it for each.

The next iteration of this will come with the planned introduction of the System Object. Please follow https://roadmaps.sap.com/board?PRODUCT=73555000100800003351&q=system%2520object&range=CURRENT-LAST#;INNO=5B28020001AC1EDE87E4A952B31A531A and the intent to use the functional aspect of the System Object to exactly allow reliability approaches based on grouped technical objects in accordance to their functional interplay.

More to come.

Greetings

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
If i am slected for SAP courses then I will be able to get a professional certificate related to my current work. I can put this course certificate in resume to get applied to a job. This course will boost my job prospects with graduation degree. It will help me to perform better in carrying out various task related to current work and give me an edge over my competitors applying IJP’s or external vacancies.. A verified certificate will attach credibility to the certificate I receive from this course. I think it will be beneficial for me to get into a good firm, But I’ve small job and not able to carry the expanses to pay for the certificate of this course. I must support family. In this circumstance, it is very much difficult for me to gather such amount of money for the certificate. Financial Aid will help me take this course without any adverse impact on my monthly essential needs. I need this course very badly for my CV and for increasing my knowledge about the subject. My current income and pocket doesn't allow me to afford this course fee. So I am badly in need of this financial aid.

interested in courses
CRM and Customer Experience
Supply Chain Management

entry.title: Re: Purchasing a discounted certification attempt
entry.content: thanks

entry.title: Re: Shared Memory Objects - Transfer data between ABAP programs
entry.content:
@Jelena_Perfiljeva : Thank you Jelena, for taking the time to reply to my question. It is a valuable insight indeed. 

Could you help me a little further to understand how exactly SAP classifies statements as 'OBSOLETE'.

I mean I have still not come to terms with SELECT INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS becoming obsolete. It was such a nice and simple syntax. The 'NEW' alternative is in fact more time consuming.

So any specific criteria based on which these statements are classified as obsolete?

entry.title: cumulative amounts
entry.content: I wanted to ask please how I can create a line graph of cumulative amounts over a period of time

entry.title: Re: Guidance on Transitioning from SAP Business One Consultant to SAP S/4HANA FICO consultant
entry.content: For sure, it's not a question about SAP Websites. Post either in https://community.sap.com/t5/career-corner-discussions/bd-p/career-cornerforum-board or in https://community.sap.com/t5/financial-management-q-a/qa-p/financial-management-questions.

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hi @David_Chaviano  

It's great to see SAP supporting cloud learning and the Digital Skills Initiative.

entry.title: Re: How to trace authorization error for a specific user...
entry.content: Hi Marco, I am not sure about the CCMS alert configuration on the missing authorization.

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Dear David,

Subject: Request for a Free SAP Certification Exam Opportunity

Dear David,

I hope this message finds you well.

As a recent graduate, I am writing to express my interest in an opportunity to take an SAP certification exam free of charge. This certification would significantly enhance my CV and help me take an important step toward becoming an SAP consultant. Besides, I am particularly keen to deepen my expertise not only in SAP functional modules such as MM and PP but also in cloud technologies, which I understand are becoming increasingly important in the SAP ecosystem. Therefore, I would be grateful if you could consider my request, and I look forward to any advice or assistance you may be able to offer.

entry.title: Re: Guidance on Transitioning from SAP Business One Consultant to SAP S/4HANA FICO consultant
entry.content: For such open questions, I'm afraid that most of people would post the answers by ChatGPT. The training documents are probably all under copyright, so you will probably get nothing without paying for a training. You may first familiarize with https://learning.sap.com, and also, for the lowest cost possible, I suggest that you log into a real S/4HANA system https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/sap-s-4hana-fully-activated-appliance-create-your-sap-s-4hana-system-in-a/ba-p/13365960.

entry.title: Re: Need to configure email alert for XS jobs in HANA
entry.content:
Are you using SAP HANA smart data integration to do replication? Is your scheduled procedure replication task or flowgraph?  If you schedule replication task/flowgraph, the help document can be used to setup the according replication task/flowgraph notification. 

Best regards
Helen

entry.title: Re: CamelJdbcAdapter OAuth Configuration for Databricks
entry.content:
There is no Oauth 2 option for the connection through Cameljdbcadapter since it is a general adapter for JDBC connection. 

Best regards
Helen

entry.title: Re: Live Data from SAP S4 Hana to Microsoft Azure
entry.content: There SDI, you can get realtime replicated data from S4 hana, but load data to "Microsoft Azure Dedicated SQL Pool"

entry.title: Re: Live Data from SAP S4 Hana to Microsoft Azure
entry.content:
Through SDI, you can get realtime replicated data from S4 hana, but load data to "Microsoft Azure Dedicated SQL Pool", you have to confirm in our PAM document if this is supported or not.
https://support.sap.com/content/dam/launchpad/en_us/pam/pam-essentials/TIP/PAM_HANA_SDI_2_0.pdf

For cloud related connection, right now we only support "Microsoft Azure Synapse Analytics" "Microsoft Azure Data Explorer (ADX) "

Best regards
Helen

entry.title: Re: How to increase limit of TCP connections in SDI
entry.content:
It is controlled by HANA parameter for TCP connection.

ALTER SYSTEM ALTER CONFIGURATION ('dpserver.ini', 'SYSTEM') SET ('framework', 'initialChannelsPerAgent') = '20' WITH  RECONFIGURE; (default is 10)


Best regards
Helen

entry.title: Re: DTP Filter restriction on single values with SDI (ODa...
entry.content:
By passing 50 single values, you mean pass filter to ODATA connection?
There might be limitation for the ODATA source, what kind of error you see? You can also confirm the same through ODATA request directly and see if it can work. 

Best regards
Helen

entry.title: Re: SAP BAS Replication Task - No source can be selected
entry.content:
Possible it is authorization issue, check our help document https://help.sap.com/docs/HANA_SMART_DATA_INTEGRATION/018757bb7f5c4700a8840976c8730f34/df2d69fe55e34406b1f8d54c43e6aee5.html?q=HDI

Best regards
Helen

entry.title: Re: Configuring OAuth 2.0 between SAP C4C and SAP BTP
entry.content:
Hi Arun,

 

I did these steps last week several times, without success. Today I found your blog entry.

As IDP we are using Azure via SSO.

When you create the destination, can you check the destination? I am getting always an 401.

I dont find the place, where my mistake might be...

Jens

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello @David_Chaviano and everyone, 

My name is Quyen, and I'm from Vietnam. I'm currently a final-year female student majoring in Information Technology.

I want to express my genuine interest in this opportunity, it provides a pathway for us who looking to begin and advance our careers through SAP certification. I recently came across SAP and was fascinated by its innovative technology solutions. Also, the SAP Learning Hub is a valuable resource for communities to learn and enhance skills. I have already enrolled in some courses on the SAP Learning and am eager to complete the learning path.

I really hope to be the part of SAP Digital Skills Initiative program and I'm truly grateful for this chance.

Thank you for your consideration and this amazing opportunity.

Best Regards, 

Quyen

entry.title: Re: Cycle counting: Configuration, Process flow and Implementation
entry.content: Thank you Rajen. The procedure is well documented and explained.

entry.title: Re: Dynamic programming fail: pass table TYPE cds as a parameter
entry.content:
Dear @Sandra_Rossi @ulrich_mhrke ,

as promised, I am coming back with the solution. The key was that, although it might seem suprising, it is possible in ABAP to type data from a variable when you use dynamic data type creation using CREATE DATA.

Situation: multiple programs calling the same generic class, passing a table name and data. The structure of tables and data differ program by program. The class displays the data in ALV (among others).

Problem:

  1. I need to declare receiving structure in the class as TYPE REF TO DATA, that is the only option. But assigning a reference or internal table to generic data type leads to an types mismatch error.
  2. I cannot use RTTS to type (describe) the internal table in the class, because the structure does not exist in dictionary. In fact, the source of data is a SELECT from a CDS entity view.
  3. I need to pass an internal table to CL_SALV_TABLE=>FACTORY CHANGING parameter, not a reference (field symbol).

Solution:

  1. Pass data and table name separately. We will end up playing with two field symbols, one for data, another for table.
  2. Wrap data to a reference and pass it to the constructor.
  3. Create a data reference for an internal table based on the provided name: CREATE DATA it_table TYPE TABLE OF (lv_name).
  4.  Create an intermediate work area that points to the dynamically created internal table: ASSIGN it_table->* TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs_it_table>).
  5. Do the same with the data: ASSIGN data->* TO <fs_data>.
  6. Use APPEND LINES OF <fs_data> TO <fs_it_table> statement to copy data from the source to the dynamically created internal table.
  7. Simply dereference the table when passing it to ALV factory.
"Calling program

DATA: go_test TYPE REF TO z_fi_interface_dynamic,
      lr_data_ref TYPE REF TO data.

" Create a data reference for the internal table t_data_obs
GET REFERENCE OF t_data_obs INTO lr_data_ref.

  TRY.
    CREATE OBJECT go_test
        EXPORTING
            data = lr_data_ref " since t_data_obs is a standard internal table, we need to wrap it in a data reference
            name = 'ZFI_DATA_OBS'.
    CATCH cx_static_check INTO DATA(lx_error_static_test).
      MESSAGE lx_error_static_test->get_text( ) TYPE 'S' DISPLAY LIKE 'E'.
  ENDTRY.

  go_test->display_alv( ).
"Generic class

CLASS z_fi_interface_dynamic DEFINITION
  PUBLIC
  FINAL
  CREATE PUBLIC .

  PUBLIC SECTION.
    METHODS: constructor
      IMPORTING
        VALUE(name) TYPE string
        VALUE(data) TYPE REF TO data,
      display_alv.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
    DATA: it_table TYPE REF TO data, " Generic data reference for dynamic typing
          lv_name  TYPE string,
          o_alv    TYPE REF TO cl_salv_table.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS z_fi_interface_dynamic IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD constructor.
    lv_name = name.

    " Create a data reference for an internal table based on the provided name
    CREATE DATA it_table TYPE TABLE OF (lv_name).

    " Assign the data reference to a field symbol for dynamic access
    FIELD-SYMBOLS: <fs_it_table> TYPE STANDARD TABLE,
                   <fs_data>     TYPE STANDARD TABLE.

    ASSIGN it_table->* TO <fs_it_table>.
    ASSIGN data->* TO <fs_data>.

    " Make sure both field symbols are assigned before proceeding
    IF sy-subrc = 0.
      " Append the lines of the provided data to the newly created internal table
      APPEND LINES OF <fs_data> TO <fs_it_table>.
    ENDIF.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD display_alv.
    TRY.
        cl_salv_table=>factory(
          IMPORTING r_salv_table = o_alv
          CHANGING  t_table      = it_table->* ).

        " Display the ALV
        o_alv->display( ).

      CATCH cx_salv_msg INTO DATA(lx_salv_msg).
        MESSAGE lx_salv_msg->get_text( ) TYPE 'E'.
    ENDTRY.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Dear David Chaviano,

My name is Hai, and I am working towards earning SAP certification to advance my career in the IT field. SAP certification is highly valued in the industry and will greatly enhance my skills and career prospects.

The voucher you are offering would be a tremendous help in making this goal more affordable and achievable. I would be very grateful for your consideration.

Thank you for your time and support.

Sincerely,
Hai

entry.title: How to send emails from Quality Notification Action Box
entry.content:
Dear all,

I am trying to trigger the sending of an email after selecting a task from the Action Box of a Quality Notification.

In particular, one of the tasks configured in the Action Box is "Send mail to supplier".

In the on-premise version of SAP S/4 Hana, when you define an activity in its customizing node, there is a field where you can associate the Function Module to trigger the sending of the mail when you select that specific task.

In SAP S/4 Hana Public Cloud, that field is missing.

Therefore I ask you kindly how I can make sure that, following the selection of the task "Send mail to supplier", in the system screen the pop-up for sending an email opens.

Thank you all.

https://youtu.be/nSwrvl182aY

entry.title: Top 10 takeaways #SAPTeched 2024
entry.content:
1. Harnessing GenAI: The True Power Lies in Its Application

"You know how they say we can only access 20% of our brain? This lets you access all of it!" That's what Vernon Gant told Eddie Morra when he gave him the NZT-48 pill in the movie Limitless. Since OpenAI released ChatGPT in November 2022, large language models (LLMs) and other foundational models have gone mainstream—giving everyone their own version of NZT-48.

The question is: What do people bring to the table? Are they like Eddie, with a solid foundation in math and law that needs a boost? Or like Gennady, whose extortion skills got turbocharged? This analogy applies to companies as well.

SAP has a solid head start with its Business Technology Platform (BTP). At TechEd, SAP announced a slew of AI-enabled advancements that would have been impossible just 23 months ago.

Screen Capture of the Movie 'limitless' Released 2011

2. Embracing Innovation Through SAP BTP

As technology evolves, building new code or introducing innovative approaches depends on a strong platform foundation. SAP has fully embraced this with its Business Technology Platform (BTP). Originally launched as SAP Cloud Platform, BTP has evolved into a unified platform that consolidates essential components to help businesses scale, innovate, and thrive.

BTP's key pillars—application development, automation, integration, data and analytics, and AI—are driving innovation across industries, enabling companies to adapt to changing needs. With 27,000 live customers and over 2,800 partners, SAP BTP's success continues to grow, empowering businesses to stay competitive.

SAP BTP Portfolio 2024

3. AI Foundation on BTP: Relevant, Reliable, Responsible

"Relevant, Reliable, Responsible" is not just a buzzword for SAP.  SAP has taken significant actions to demonstrate this commitment.

  • Responsible: The AI Foundation model is expanding access to more LLMs. A highlight this year is Mistral Large 2, which will be hosted in SAP's own data centers rather than relying on external APIs, ensuring complete control over your data. Additionally, SAP has announced access to the latest LLMs from Aleph Alpha, Meta, and IBM.
  • Reliable: The SAP Gen AI Hub now offers various orchestration tools—such as data masking, grounding, and content filtering—to ensure that outputs from LLMs are trustworthy and less prone to hallucinations.
  • Relevant: SAP has built a comprehensive set of AI services, AI lifecycle management, and business data and content for AI, allowing seamless integration into SAP applications—all accessible via Joule.

SAP AI Foundation

4. Joule Updates: Your Trusted Co-Pilot

Joule, SAP's intelligent co-pilot, turns one this year! It will cover 80% of commonly used workflows and tasks in SAP, featuring 500 skills today with a goal to reach 1,200 by year-end, spanning HR, finance, sales, supply chain, and more.

New announcements include:

  • Multiple Language Support: Joule now supports multiple languages, expanding its accessibility.
  • Expanded Data Centers: Increased infrastructure to support Joule's growing capabilities.
  • Availability in SAP Mobile Start: Joule is now accessible in SAP Mobile Start.
  • Joule Studio in SAP Build: Enabling two million ABAP developers globally, Joule now supports ABAP developer capabilities in Eclipse.

Future Outlook:

Joule Agents: Specialized in specific domains like HR or Customer service, these agents leverage multi-agent systems, allowing businesses to create custom skills, including integration with other enterprise apps like ServiceNow via APIs. This expansion ensures Joule remains a powerful co-pilot, enabling seamless transactional, informational, and analytical interactions to boost business efficiency and automation.

Joule Agents

5. Unified Application Development Under SAP Build, Including ABAP

Two years ago, SAP introduced a low-code environment and last year added AI-powered code development for Java and JavaScript. This year, the latest SAP Build release includes ABAP Cloud, which comes with its own large language model trained on 25 million lines of ABAP code, offering immense power for the SAP ecosystem.

What does this mean? You can now extend your core S/4HANA systems using an AI-powered approach in ABAP Cloud in two ways:

  • On-Stack: Build custom extensions directly within the core system.
  • Side-by-Side: Develop modular, customizable extensions on SAP BTP.

The key is to maintain a clean core. The goal is to make all applications lifecycle-compliant, meaning they are built to last without requiring adaptations during innovation cycles. This applies to both private and public cloud. For private cloud, SAP Build supports RFC connectivity, enabling seamless third-party integration with S/4HANA. For public cloud, there is the SAP Build Extensibility Wizard, which helps key users and developers create both on-stack and side-by-side extensions within S/4HANA in an embedded experience.

ABAP Cloud in SAP Build

6. Data in the GenAI Age

In the era of Generative AI, creating intelligent applications requires careful data handling. While foundational models perform well, enterprise data needs precise management.

  • HANA Cloud Vector Engine: enables in-database vectorization with a proprietary SAP-specific text model, simplifying the creation of vector embeddings. This allows LLMs to provide more accurate, context-aware outputs for enterprise use. A notable example is Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), where the engine retrieves relevant business data to improve AI results.
  • Knowledge Graph Engine: This engine organizes data as interconnected facts, helping users navigate relationships and extract insights easily. In the GenAI space, knowledge graphs can power domain-specific question-answering systems, combining business data from manuals, documents, and records to generate precise, context-aware responses.
  • GenAI Toolkit for HANA Cloud: Similar to ABAP Cloud's AI-enabled code generation, this toolkit provides users access via Joule, allowing them to query datasets and receive results through AI-driven interactions.

SAP Knowledge Graph

7. Analytics in the GenAI age

Both SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud have received significant enhancements this year.

  • SAP Analytics Cloud now features SAP Analytics Cloud Compass, set to be generally available in Q1 2025. This feature enables business users to model complex risk scenarios using the Monte Carlo simulation method. With a user-friendly interface, non-technical users can perform real-time risk analysis without needing prior setup or technical expertise.
  • SAP Datasphere gets a boost with new embedded data lake capabilities, including the Integrated Object Store for storing large amounts of data, Spark Data Processing capabilities, and SQL Files for live access to data in the integrated object store without duplication. These features allow organizations of all sizes to implement a business data fabric in a simple, cost-efficient, and scalable way, powered by SAP HANA Cloud.

SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datashpere

8. SAP Integration Suite Enhancements

Many SAP customers leveraging SAP Integration Suite have experienced a significant return on investment—up to 345%, according to Forrester.

In the era of Generative AI, you can now optimize your existing integrations with AI-generated recommendations. This allows you to run script optimizations, modify connections, relaunch applications, and immediately observe the changes in system performance after the optimizations, providing a significant boost. (This feature will be available as part of SAP Integration Suite Premium Edition.)

Additionally, there's a new commercial offering: the SAP Integration Suite Starter Edition for SAP SuccessFactors and SAP GROW customers. This edition provides access to all pre-packaged SAP integrations, along with a fixed number of customizable integrations.

SAP Integration Suite

9. Business Transformation Management

LeanIX, Signavio, and WalkMe now form a cohesive block where customers need it most—business transformation management. With LeanIX, you can automatically discover your current application landscape. Signavio helps you discover and design your business processes, and WalkMe enables people to seamlessly navigate and adopt these processes, supporting activities such as employee onboarding, performance management, and job changes in SAP SuccessFactors.

SAP Business Transformation Management

10. AI Use Cases in Action Across Applications

A sample customer, "TeachIT Technologies," was used to demonstrate the power of Generative AI in various application spaces. The scenario: TeachIT Technologies, a mid-sized software implementation company, is facing challenges in project management, resource allocation, and international expansion.

  • Cloud ERP: Project managers track success criteria with GenAI-powered in-app extensibility. SAP BTP allows for custom application development, streamlining processes without manual coding.
  • UI and AI-Assisted Development: Developers use SAP Fiori Tools and LLMs to quickly transform sketches into functional applications, simplifying the development process (future-looking).
  • Global Expansion & Compliance: SAP Central Business Configuration (CBC) and GenAI automatically configure regional business processes for expansion, ensuring compliance with local regulations. AI in SAP’s compliance tools resolves tax errors like incorrect GST numbers.
  • Sales & Operations: SAP Sales Cloud and SuccessFactors use AI tools to help project managers manage teams, set goals, and track budgets, making decision-making easier.

Almost all applications, in one form or another, have their own 'NZT-48 pill'!

Please explore these in the newly launched SAP Business AI Features in SAP Discovery center.

https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/index.html

SAP Business AI Features

entry.title: Re: Change the file name of a Custom Form Template PDF (S...
entry.content:
Hi Dominik,

as far as I know, it is only possible via ABSL Coding and I am not sure if this matches your use case.

I once implemented a solution with a Custom BO using Attachment Folder BO. Therefor I added the created PDF as attachment to the UI, then you are able to set a custom docName.

entry.title: Re: Report/Dashboard tracking links between task,requirem...
entry.content: To add to Robin's comment, they have recently added Features under Indirect Assignment on the Solution Process Traceability Report. Adding it should give you a single pane of glass from Scope to Requirement, to User Story/Task, Feature, Test Case, Test Execution, and Defect (i.e. and End-to-End Traceability Report)

entry.title: Re: Stores and Attachments - AppGyver Solution - Low-Code No-Code Challenge
entry.content: Hey can you please provide more detail on your screenshots, I'm having a little hard time following, Thanks

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello @David_Chaviano 

I'm a final year student and in great ambition to become a SAP consultant, especially S/4 Hana.

I have completed multiple free courses and had records of achievement, however can not have an associate certificate due to financial situation.

I really want to upgrade my knowledge of SAP technology and get my first job in SAP field soon.

Thank you for this opportunity.

entry.title: How to Migrate the Existing Workflow to SAP Business Process Automation
entry.content:
How to Migrate the Existing Workflow to BPA

Reason for the Topic: The goal is to outline the procedures and challenges we encountered, along with the solutions that successfully addressed those challenges, making it helpful for beginners eager to learn about BPA.

Pre-Requisites:

Step 1: Go to Subaccount → Go to Instances and Subscriptions → Click on "Create" and then select "SAP Build Process Automation Service" with the free plan. (This will add a BPA subscription.)

 

 

 

 

Next Steps: Creating an Instance for BPA Features

The next target is to create an instance to utilize the features of BPA for our requirements.

Steps:

  1. Go to Subaccount.
  2. Navigate to Instances and Subscriptions.
  3. Click on Create, then select Build Process Automation Service with the standard plan.
  4. Enter a name for the instance. (You can use this instance name in your processes or workflows to access BPA.)

 

 

This instance is essentially an object you’re creating to utilize the features of BPA.

I hope this clarifies why we are creating an instance for these services.

Step 2:

2.1  Go to Subaccount.

2.2  Navigate to Security.

2.3  Click on Users.

2.4Select Assign Role Collection and choose Process Automation Admin, Developer,and Participant.

 

 

 

 

You will now have access to utilize the BPA features.

Migrating the Existing Workflow to BPA

If your requirement is to move the existing workflow to BPA, here are the steps to follow:

Step 3:

Create a Dev Space and import the existing .mtar file into your project folder.

 

 

Steps:

  1. Modify the mta.yaml file as follows:

_schema-version: "3.2"
ID: FLOWID
version: 0.0.1
modules:
- name: Flow
  type: com.sap.application.content
  path: Flow
  requires:
    - name: BPA_Instance
      parameters:
        content-target: true
        service-key:
          config:
            deployUsageScenario: workflow
          name: spa-workflow-service-key
resources:
- name: BPA_Instance
  type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
  parameters:
    service: process-automation-service
    service-plan: standard

 

 

Explanation of the mta.yaml File

In the mta.yaml file, BPA_Instance is the instance name that we created in Step 1.

 In the resources section, we specify the service name and the service plan that we used.

This configuration indicates that we are utilizing the BPA instance created, effectively migrating the workflow to BPA.

If the deployment is successful, you will see your deployed flow in the Monitor Workflows section.

 

 

Issues You May Face

Issue 1: Workflow is Not Visible as Deployed in Process Visibility

Resolution:

  • Go to the service instance and check whether the flow is bound to your instance. You can verify this in the service key.
  • Navigate to View Credentials in the instance. You should see the client ID and secret. If you don’t see the content endpoint, there may be an issue with the mta.yaml file.
  • Double-check the format of the mta.yaml file to ensure everything is correct.

 

To Monitor the Workflow in Process Visibility

Import the existing Process Visibility from Standard in the Projects section of BPA. After that, you can release and deploy the project.

Issues You May Face

Issue: You will not be able to see the workflow in BPA Process Visibility.

Resolution: First, check whether your workflow is visible in Monitor Workflows and if you can see your deployed workflow. If not, refer to the solution for Issue 1 and see if that resolves the problem.

Key Points Covered:

  • Migrating existing workflows to BPA.
  • Creating an instance in BPA.
  • Modifying the mta.yaml file.
  • Monitoring workflows.
  • Issues encountered and their solutions.

" Thanks for following along! Stay tuned for my next post, where I will dive into how to trigger workflow APIs from the service. Your journey with BPA continues!"

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hi David,

My name is Abdelrahman Atwany, and I am a SAP FI/CO consultant at ECC. I identify as someone who faces challenges in accessing equal opportunities due to my geographic location, as well as financial constraints that prevent me from affording a certification attempt for myself.

I have recently completed my first learning journey, Overview of Financials in SAP S/4HANA. This initiative by SAP has been an incredible opportunity for me, and I am eager to further enhance my skills through formal certification.

I would greatly appreciate your consideration of my request for a certification voucher, which would significantly impact my professional development.

Thank you for your time, and I look forward to your response.

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hi @David_Chaviano 

My name is Merlin, and I am from India. I would like to begin by expressing my gratitude to SAP for this initiative aimed at including individuals from generally underrepresented groups.

I hold a postgraduate degree in Finance and have 5 years of experience as a financial analyst. However, I took a break from my finance career two years ago. During this time, my growing interest in technology and the rapid advancements in the IT sector have inspired me to consider a career transition to IT. I believe that SAP provides the ideal opportunity to make this shift. As a woman seeking to transition from finance to IT, I am excited to qualify for the SAP Digital Skills initiative.


Thank you in advance .

Merlin Mathew

entry.title: HOW to create a custom LOGIN page in CAPM in sap BTP
entry.content:
Hello experts 

I am working on SAP BTP .  have created a full stack project  By using combinations of technologies such as CAPM(cloud application programming model), CDS(core data services),node JS ,FIORI ui5,additionaly  added authentication using xsuaa which secures my odata services   then deployed it over cloudfoundry  and it is working correctly.

i followed this tutorial    :     

Set Up Trust Between SAP Cloud Identity Services and SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry environment | SAP Tutorials 

and setup trust between cloud identity services and sap BTP ,cloudfoundry  environment . I understands this provides Authentication and single sign-on for users in the cloud.  

 

 After clicking on URL(Which is in my cloud foundry space) it redirects me to a Login page which is default login page of Idp(identity providers)  and after authenticating users ,xsuaa server and approuter exchanges token and then approuter, routes   on my  application welcome page (or any xml view in fiori ui5 which i have used for welcome page) .                                                                                                                                            This is the default login page of Idp  which i have faced:

Is it is possible that i can create my own custom login page instead of using default login page of Idp by keeping the functionality of approuter, and xsuaa server as same .

i explored sap community and got some usefull tutorials and resources which helps me in understanding the flow of login and working of browser, approuter, xsuaa server, idp  .                                                                      These resources are here:

  Guide for User Authentication and Authorization in... - SAP Community

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-application-router/ba-p/13393550

Could you please guide me on How to create custom login page for login by keeping the functionality of approuter and xsuaa server as same  .

Any recommendations, tutorial, or resources  specific to SAP BTP  would be greatly appreciated.

@gregorw

@thomas_jung 

@MSo 

 @AlexPJ  @RobertWaywell 

 @MioYasutake

 @qmacro

@gregorw

@Animatron56

@martinstenzig 

@Dinu 

@Bibhu 

@eshrinivasan 

@Dan_Wroblewski 

@David_Chaviano 

@acancelado 

@Senqi_Hu 

@DominikTylczyn

entry.title: Re: Consuming WebServices directly from ABAP
entry.content: Pretty good. A friendly suggestion: if you ever feel like extending this tutorial, you may want to show something about mapping values to the message and dealing with types mismatch. Also catching the response and error-handling gets handy for beginners. Configuring timeout and retry is also important. Actually, there is so much to write about... 😄

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello David @David_Chaviano 

I identify as a person in the below categories:

  • Women
  • Single parents
  • People looking to switch their careers mid-life
  • People who lack the financial means to afford a certification attempt for themselves

I am interest in participating in the digital skills initiative and learn more about SAP. I have started my first learning journey Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Thank you,

Tre B

entry.title: My SAP journey begins ...
entry.content:
Hi David,

I’m Divya, a Chemical Engineer currently working in Trade Finance. As a woman aiming to break into the advanced tech industry, I’ve encountered my share of challenges. However, I see this opportunity to explore SAP as a pivotal step in overcoming those barriers and expanding my expertise. I’m confident that my background in engineering and finance will allow me to bring a unique perspective to SAP solutions, and I’m eager to learn and grow alongside this community. Thank you for this opportunity, and I look forward to connecting , contributing and getting certified in SAP Module.

Best regards,

Divya

entry.title: Re: BUSINESS PLACE IN SD DOCUMENT
entry.content:
Hello Santosh,

Thank you for your reply. With "Assign business place to plants" the business place is getting up in the Billing Document. But I want to generate the business place from "Assign business place to Sales office", is it possible to have it with this config for billing documents related to country India?

entry.title: Re: The Love-Hate Relationship with Networking
entry.content:
I love your take on networking! It’s refreshing and highlights something often overlooked: networking isn’t about quantity but quality. Building deeper, authentic connections has far more impact than trying to meet everyone. I also agree that it’s draining when you feel the pressure to "perform" or be someone you're not. It’s key to be yourself—genuine conversations are where real growth happens.

Stepping outside your comfort zone is necessary, but it doesn’t mean you need to sacrifice authenticity. In the end, it’s about finding those few meaningful connections that truly help your professional journey.

And ja I definitely have my fair share of awkward networking moments where once introduced myself to someone at an event, thinking we’d never met before, and I went on talking about how excited I was to connect. Halfway through, they politely reminded me we had already met at a previous conference and had even worked together on a small project! 😅 Safe to say, I felt pretty embarrassed, but we ended up laughing about it. Those moments definitely stay with you! 😊

entry.title: FORM 27EQ - TCS POSTING KEY
entry.content:
Hello all,

I am having an issue with the India TCS FORM 27EQ. While generating the "Remittance Challan - Customers", I am getting an error message saying, "No unpaid Tax item exists for given selection screen".

I am doing the TCS using Sales document with condition type "JGTC" which is assigned to withholding tax type Y6 and withholding tax code 66. TCS is getting debited in the sales document (Posting Key - 40). Can anyone tell me if it is the right entry or not? If it is not a correct entry, can someone please guide me how can I change the posting key in sap public cloud.

One more question, if I am doing the report with TCS Tax codes (JH - Final TCS and JI - Interim tax code) then the Remittance is getting created and the report is working fine. The TCS entry in FI document in this case is credited with posting key 50.

Can you please highlight what is the correct way to do this report. Is it via WHT tax types and tax codes or via TCS tax codes? 

I am looking forward for good answers and quick answers as this is something very urgent.

Thank you

entry.title: Re: Exporting a simulation as a mp4
entry.content:
Hi Emmanuel,

Sorry, I don't have an answer for this issue but it is relatively common.  I suspect that it is something to do with Microsoft Codec used to generate the video file but I'm not certain.  I've checked the knowledge base and there is nothing on this topic so I recommend that you raise a support ticket using the Component ID KM-SEN-PRO and get support involved.

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Re: Turn on "Show Navigation Arrows" in all Book pages in...
entry.content:
Hi Shaun,

For you existing content, try adding modifying the setting on your Book Page templates.  If you haven't modified the corresponding setting on individual Book Pages, applying the setting to the Book Page templates should updating the corresponding setting on the individual Book Pages.

Regards
Shane
